evidence_ref: RESOLVED (user-directed, 2026-06-21): doctor + honest-status now thread explicit_root=bool(args.root) into read_version/default_repo_slug (the same anchor-resolution pattern as version/update --check), so an installed doctor/honest-status reports the install-anchor identity (version + DYAI2025/Plumbline slug) from ANY cwd, never the foreign cwd's VERSION/origin. Proven offline in config/claude/tests/test_update_layer.sh (283/0, the FOLLOWUP-DOCTOR block: installed doctor/honest-status from /tmp + a foreign repo report 0.22.0 + DYAI2025/Plumbline, never 9.9.9/EVILFORK); explicit --root still reads the given root; a dev checkout (no anchor) still falls through to root/VERSION+origin. doctor's load_compatibility was wrapped (try/except) so off-tree the version/slug lines print while a genuine on-tree compatibility failure still surfaces via the independent `compatibility.json: fail` check (proven by 2 code-reviews, 4 on-tree cases).
note: integration-fake (offline anchor-resolution; sandbox $CLAUDE_HOME, real ~/.claude never written). NEW tracked follow-up (named, not silently dropped): doctor's FILE-existence checks (install.sh/VERSION/run_all.sh/compatibility.json) still read against cwd for an installed COPY (which lays out bin/lib under $CLAUDE_HOME, not the repo structure), so they report `fail` from the install home -- pre-existing, out of this REQ's scope; the contracted version/slug surface is now correct regardless. A separate, larger change if a future REQ wants doctor's file-checks meaningful on an installed copy.

evidence_ref: LIVE (real-boundary-smoke -- what actually crossed the boundary): a real `update --check` against api.github.com on 2026-06-21 returned local 0.21.0 / latest 0.21.0 / source github:DYAI2025/Plumbline / status up-to-date (rc=0) -- the GitHub release-API boundary was crossed (unauth path, no token set, no leak). OFFLINE (integration-fake -- everything else stays at its honest class): config/claude/tests/test_update_layer.sh PUR-3 block (115/0) + config/claude/lib/plumbline_update.py fetch_latest_release/_github_token -- token-aware fetch (GITHUB_TOKEN->GH_TOKEN->gh auth token; an explicitly-empty env var is an explicit unauth opt-out), 403 rate-limited classified DISTINCTLY from 404 not-found (both escape the generic wrapper), token NEVER logged on success/403/404 (raise from None), slug validated against path-injection. SECURITY (Gate-B PASS): the Authorization Bearer header is sent ONLY to api.github.com unless an explicit DEFAULT-OFF gate is set -- CRITICAL-1 token-exfil fixed; the exfil falsifier reddens on regression, proven by revert.
note: The token-auth path itself was NOT live-exercised (the live --check ran unauth) -- it is integration-fake; only the fetch/version-compare crossed the real boundary. CAVEAT (Gate-B): PLUMBLINE_GITHUB_API_ALLOW_INSECURE_TOKEN=1 is a deliberate DEFAULT-OFF test-only/operator-debug escape allowing the token to a non-github host -- it must NEVER be set in a shipped/CI env carrying a real token. A re-run of the unauth --check may rate-limit; Sprint-2 added the token path to raise the limit.

evidence_ref: config/claude/tests/test_update_layer.sh: _apply_into_home snapshots the whole $CLAUDE_HOME (shutil.copytree into .plumbline/update/snapshots, ignoring .git/.plumbline/__pycache__) BEFORE the real installer runs; on an INJECTED verify-failure it restores the WHOLE home (prior files back, anchor to vN, added files removed) and exits non-zero -- proven live against a real sandbox $CLAUDE_HOME. Revert is faithful to ignored-but-present entries (CR-3: a planted __pycache__ survives), and prints the `plumbline rollback <snapshot>` recovery command (CR-4). A failed update never leaves a half-updated home.
verified_by: agileteam tester+coder+security(Gate-B PASS: snapshot taken before payload runs, payload cannot poison it)+2 code-reviews, 2026-06-21
note: SANDBOX $CLAUDE_HOME only. Crash mid-revert is non-atomic but recoverable via the printed `plumbline rollback` (CR-4) -- documented, not silent.

evidence_ref: code-review follow-up (LOW, NOT reachable through the production composition root): install.sh same_path() uses `cd && pwd -P`, which only resolves DIRECTORIES; for two FILE paths both cd's fail and pwd is empty, so same_path returns true for any two file paths. Insulated in prod by the --copy hardwire + the CR-1 symlink refusal, so it does not cause a wrong refresh/skip via `plumbline update` (copy refresh uses cmp -s; symlink installs are refused). Recorded as a tracked follow-up; hardening: require directory inputs / realpath files, and use a readlink-based compare in content_current's symlink branch.
verified_by: agileteam re-review (2 reviewers concur: low + unreachable), 2026-06-21
note: Surfaced explicitly per the truth-through-awareness stance -- a known latent quirk, governable, not hidden. Also tracked: a SEC-3 non-https-tarball test and a CR-4 recover-line assertion (closed in the Sprint-3 coverage pass).
